title: A CDN is an eventually-consistent cache
description: Edge PoPs cache independently; a purge propagates over time, so global consistency after a change is not instant.
body: Use when reasoning about staleness after a content change. Each edge location caches independently and a purge reaches PoPs over seconds, so different users may briefly see old and new content. Do not assume a change is globally live the instant you deploy or purge. Actions: design for brief inconsistency, use versioned URLs for must-be-fresh assets, and measure purge propagation. Validation signals include observed propagation time within tolerance. Risk boundary: content requiring strict consistency (legal, pricing) should be versioned or not edge-cached.
